status_detail: Planned 500 MW (expandable to 1 GW) green hydrogen and derivatives project in Thierbach, Saxony, Germany. Developed by HH2E. Alkaline electrolysis. Producing hydrogen, e-methanol, and other power-to-X products. 2028-2030 COD. Brownfield site reusing former power plant infrastructure.

data_management.narrative_summary: HH2E is developing the Hydrogen City project in Thierbach, Saxony, Germany, with 500 MW of initial alkaline electrolysis capacity expandable to 1 GW. The brownfield site reuses infrastructure from a former power plant, including grid connection, cooling water, and buildings. The project will produce green hydrogen for industrial users and convert hydrogen to e-methanol and other power-to-X products for transport and chemical industry decarbonization. HH2E is backed by HydrogenOne Capital and targets commercial operation in 2028-2030. The project is one of the largest planned alkaline electrolysis facilities in Germany.

power.grid_connection_detail: Grid-connected at brownfield power plant site. Reusing existing infrastructure.

offtake.offtaker_name: Industrial and transport customers via power-to-X products

sources.title: HH2E Hydrogen City — 500 MW P2X project in Thierbach, Saxony
sources.author: HH2E / HydrogenOne Capital
